methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Overview Energy is a seed-stage space and aerospace company founded by Marc Berte and Andrew Cantino that has raised $20M to date. The relevant comp universe spans 5 public players (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Blue Origin, though direct peer valuation data at the seed stage remains sparse in this sector.

Stage-progression analysis, the sole applicable triangulation method, uses the $20M total raised as a proxy and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement in venture. This yields an implied valuation of $50M. Peer-median analysis proved inapplicable due to insufficient comparable datapoints at the same or adjacent stage—while 23 companies operate in Space & Aerospace broadly, fewer than three meet narrow stage and tier filters. Public-comp anchoring is also inapplicable: the Space & Aerospace public median trades at 64.2x price-to-sales across the five tickers identified, but Overview Energy's revenue is not disclosed, preventing calculation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $42M to $57M, centered on $50M. This range reflects low-to-high bounds around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue data (limiting public-comp validation), the thinness of seed-stage private comparables in this sector, and unknown burn rate or capital efficiency relative to peers. A post-seed financing would provide clearer market pricing.
